# Lists

## Unordered Lists

- Item 1
- Item 2
- Item 3

* Item with asterisk
* Another item

+ Item with plus
+ Another item

## Ordered Lists

1. First item
2. Second item
3. Third item

1. All items
1. Can use
1. The same number

100. Starting from 100
101. Next item
102. And another

## Nested Lists

- Level 1
  - Level 2
    - Level 3
      - Level 4
        - Level 5
          - Level 6

1. Ordered level 1
   1. Ordered level 2
      1. Ordered level 3
         1. Ordered level 4

- Mixed nesting
  1. Ordered inside unordered
     - Unordered inside ordered
       1. Back to ordered

## Task Lists

- [x] Completed task
- [ ] Incomplete task
- [x] Another completed task
  - [x] Nested completed
  - [ ] Nested incomplete
    - [ ] Deeply nested task

## Lists with Content

- **Bold item** with description

  Paragraph inside list item.

  Another paragraph.

- Item with `inline code`

- Item with a [link](https://example.com)

- Item with image: ![alt](https://via.placeholder.com/20)

1. Ordered item with code block:

   ```javascript
   console.log("Hello from a list!");
   ```

2. Ordered item with blockquote:

   > This is a quote inside a list

3. Ordered item with table:

   | A | B |
   |---|---|
   | 1 | 2 |

## Loose vs Tight Lists

- Tight item 1
- Tight item 2
- Tight item 3

- Loose item 1

- Loose item 2

- Loose item 3

## Definition Lists (if supported)

Term 1
: Definition 1a
: Definition 1b

Term 2
: Definition 2
